message:
fix: harden mission lifecycle handling (#5929-#5934) (#5939)

Bundled fixes for six related mission lifecycle bugs:

- #5929: transient WS disconnect no longer fails running missions.
  onclose/onerror now mark missions with needsReconnect and only
  transition to failed when reconnect attempts are exhausted.
- #5930: reconnect no longer blindly replays the last prompt.
  Missions track a reconnectAttempted flag — a second disconnect
  during the same mission fails it rather than re-sending the
  prompt, preventing duplicate execution on non-idempotent agents.
  Reconnect requests are also tagged with a deterministic
  resumeKey so backends can de-duplicate.
- #5931: pending missions are no longer stuck after a page reload.
  They are now failed on hydration with a clear "please retry"
  message (the backend never received the request, so resuming
  would risk a duplicate).
- #5932: pending and blocked missions can now be cancelled. The
  cancel/terminate button in MissionChat and MissionListItem is
  visible for pending/blocked states, and cancelMission
  short-circuits to a clean 'failed' transition when the mission
  has never been sent to the backend.
- #5933: blocked missions are now surfaced in the attention
  indicator badge (was only counting waiting_input and failed).
- #5934: the resolution flow now validates mission state before
  applying. handleApplyResolution rejects apply on blocked,
  pending, cancelling, and running missions; sendMessage also
  guards against the blocked state as a second layer of defense.

Test updates:
- WS close test now asserts the mission remains running with
  needsReconnect=true rather than being failed.
- loadMissions pending preservation test now asserts the mission
  is failed on reload with a recovery message.
- timeout interval pending-safety test was migrated to
  waiting_input since pending is no longer persisted across
  reloads.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
